notes: Always wrap conditional renders — exit animations won't fire without it
key prop is critical — FM uses it to detect additions/removals
Add to any element that changes size/position — FM auto-animates the reflow

code_snippet: <motion.div layoutId='card-123'/> — same layoutId in both views
notes: IDs must be identical and unique — works across AnimatePresence boundaries
Tie any motion value to scroll position — extremely powerful for parallax

code_snippet: const ref = useRef(); const inView = useInView(ref{once:true{amount:0.3}); return <motion.div ref={ref} animate={inView?'visible':'hidden'}/>
notes: Cleaner than whileInView for complex conditions
Parent controls timing — children just define their own hidden/visible variants
Pick by feel: gentle=menus snappy=buttons bouncy=playful smooth=modals
Both whileHover and whileTap can coexist — FM resolves priority automatically
mode='wait' prevents two pages rendering simultaneously

code_snippet: const {scrollYProgress}=useScroll(); <motion.div style={{scaleX:scrollYProgress{transformOrigin:'left'}} className='fixed top-0 h-1 bg-brand w-full'/>
notes: One of the most impressive effects with 3 lines of code
Bypasses React re-renders — use for performance-critical animations

code_snippet: transition={{delay:0.2}} on individual elements OR delayChildren on parent variant
notes: delay on variants relative to parent — delayChildren offsets all children
Always check — WCAG 2.3.3 requires respecting prefers-reduced-motion
